topStory.title: Show HN: AI memory with biological decay (52% recall)

stories.title: Show HN: AI memory with biological decay (52% recall)
stories.storyText: Most RAG setups fail because they treat memory like a static filing cabinet. When every transient bug fix or abandoned rule is stored forever, the context window eventually chokes on noise, spiking token costs and degrading the agent's reasoning. This implementation experiments with a biological approach by using the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve to manage context as a living substrate. Memories are assigned a "strength" score where each recall reinforces the data and flattens its decay curve (spa

topStory.title: Show HN: Browser Harness – Gives LLM freedom to complete any browser task

stories.title: Show HN: Browser Harness – Gives LLM freedom to complete any browser task
stories.storyText: Hey HN, We got tired of browser frameworks restricting the LLM, so we removed the framework and gave the LLM maximum freedom to do whatever it's trained on. We gave the harness the ability to self correct and add new tools if the LLM wants (is pre-trained on) that. Our Browser Use library is tens of thousands of lines of deterministic heuristics wrapping Chrome (CDP websocket). Element extractors, click helpers, target managemenet (SUPER painful), watchdogs (crash handling, file downloads, alert

topStory.title: Show HN: VT Code – Rust TUI coding agent with multi-provider support

stories.title: Show HN: VT Code – Rust TUI coding agent with multi-provider support
stories.storyText: Hi HN, I built VT Code, a semantic coding agent. Supports all SOTA and open sources model. Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Codex. Agent Skills, Model Context Protocol and Agent Client Protocol (ACP) ready. All open source models are support. Local inference via LM Studio and Ollama (experiment). Semantic context understanding is supported by ast-grep for structured code search and ripgrep for powered grep. I built VT Code in Rust on Ratatui. Architecture and agent loop documented in the README and De

topStory.title: Show HN: Agent Vault – Open-source credential proxy and vault for agents

stories.title: Show HN: Agent Vault – Open-source credential proxy and vault for agents
stories.storyText: Hey HN! Today we're launching Agent Vault - an open source HTTP credential proxy and vault for AI agents. Repo is at https://github.com/Infisical/agent-vault , and there's an in-depth description at https://infisical.com/blog/agent-vault-the-open-source-crede... . We built Agent Vault in response to a question that been plaguing the industry: How do we give agents secure access to services without them reading any secrets? Most teams building agents have run into this exact problem: They build a

topStory.title: Show HN: We built a <60ms, open-source alternative to E2B using RustVMM and KVM

stories.title: Show HN: We built a <60ms, open-source alternative to E2B using RustVMM and KVM
stories.storyText: Over the past few months, as we scaled our internal AI Agents, we hit a dead end: Running LLM-generated arbitrary code in Docker is basically running naked on security due to container escape risks. But using full traditional VMs takes minutes to boot and eats too much memory to support high-density concurrency. We loved the developer experience of SaaS sandboxes on the market, but they are closed-source, expensive, and have too high a barrier to entry for self-hosting. So, our team decided to b

topStory.title: Show HN: Tolaria – Open-source macOS app to manage Markdown knowledge bases

stories.title: Show HN: Tolaria – Open-source macOS app to manage Markdown knowledge bases
stories.storyText: Hey there! I am Luca, I write https://refactoring.fm/ and I built Tolaria for myself to manage my own knowledge base (10K notes, 300+ articles written in over 6 years of newslettering) and work well with AI. Tolaria is offline-first, file-based, has first-class support for git, and has strong opinions about how you should organize notes (types, relationships, etc). Let me know your thoughts!

topStory.title: Show HN: GoModel – an open-source AI gateway in Go

stories.title: Show HN: GoModel – an open-source AI gateway in Go
stories.storyText: Hi, I’m Jakub, a solo founder based in Warsaw. I’ve been building GoModel since December with a couple of contributors. It's an open-source AI gateway that sits between your app and model providers like OpenAI, Anthropic or others. I built it for my startup to solve a few problems: - track AI usage and cost per client or team - switch models without changing app code - debug request flows more easily - reduce AI spendings with exact and semantic caching How is it different? - ~17MB docker image

topStory.title: Show HN: Git Push No-Mistakes

stories.title: Show HN: Git Push No-Mistakes
stories.storyText: no-mistakes is how I kill AI slop. It puts a local git proxy in front of my real remote. I push to no-mistakes instead of origin, and it spins up a disposable worktree, runs my coding agent as a validation pipeline, forwards upstream only after every check passes, opens a clean PR automatically, and babysits CI pipeline for me.
